summary: Built proof surfaces for Lido, OpenServ, and AgentCash with 13 new files totaling 3139 lines. Lido: treasury monitor with dry-run position snapshots, stETH/wstETH balances, APR estimates, and chain config evidence. OpenServ: workflow registration tied to agent ERC-8004 identity with x402 pricing. AgentCash: wallet payment flow with x402 invoice linkage and balance snapshots. All three record evidence through the shared execution-log infrastructure.

strengths: All three integrations follow the same well-established orchestrated-flow pattern
Lido buildPositionSnapshot uses deterministic seed from treasury address for consistent dry-run results
OpenServ ties registrations to agent identity and resolves ERC-8004 data for each registration
AgentCash links payments to x402 invoices and counts existing paid invoices for wallet context
Comprehensive error handling across all flows with evidence recording for failures
Tests are thorough: 17 Lido + 13 OpenServ + 14 AgentCash tests covering success, failure, config, and edge cases

issues.description: Lido, AgentCash, and Celo all use similar deterministic dry-run snapshot builders based on address seeds. This is appropriate for proof surfaces but the pattern is duplicated.
issues.suggestion: Future refactoring could extract a shared deterministic-snapshot utility.

testCoverage.notes: 17 tests for lido-treasury-monitor + 13 tests for openserv-workflow-registration + 14 tests for agentcash-wallet-payment. All cover config loading, successful flows, error paths, and evidence recording.
